parameters.shapes_file_used_locally: patched copy of tasks/croissant-tasks-shapes.ttl (4 fixes applied — see upstream_issues)
parameters.shapes_file_shipped_in_repo: tasks/croissant-tasks-shapes.ttl (UNPATCHED upstream version — this PR does not modify it)
parameters.validator_note: Per spec-discipline convention from PR #1025, this PR does not ship modified shapes. Files validate against the patched shapes (see results below); against the unpatched upstream shapes both files FAIL with the load errors listed under upstream_issues.

stages.name: setup
stages.message: pyshacl 0.31.0 + rdflib installed; shapes + ontology downloaded from PR #1017 commit 02b87497; shapes file patched locally for pyshacl compatibility (patched copy NOT shipped in this PR)

stages.name: paper_analysis
stages.message: Paper read (10 pages); CoRe benchmark extracted with 3 task types, 2 query formulations, 3 subtasks; 1 Gemini baseline (Gemini 2.5 Pro) extracted from Table 4

stages.name: problem_generated
stages.message: core_problem.jsonld written; TaskProblem with OutputSpec, EvaluationSpec, and 3 subtasks (data_dependency, control_dependency, information_flow)

stages.name: json_validity
stages.message: All .jsonld files parse as valid JSON

stages.name: shacl_conformance_against_patched_shapes
stages.message: All files conform to the locally-patched Croissant Tasks SHACL shapes (pyshacl 0.31.0, inference=rdfs)

stages.name: shacl_conformance_against_unpatched_shapes
stages.message: All files FAIL against the unpatched upstream shapes shipped at tasks/croissant-tasks-shapes.ttl. Failure modes are deterministic shape-load errors (not constraint violations) — see upstream_issues. Symptoms match Luis's Bug A and Bug B in PR #1025 plus two additional findings.

fixes_applied_to_file: Changed croissant:dataType values from plain strings to @id IRIs (e.g. {"@id": "xsd:boolean"})
Converted all croissant:value numeric literals to xsd:string (JSON strings) to match EvaluationResultShape

upstream_issues.symptom: sh:property blank node missing sh:path (body is an sh:or) — pyshacl rejects it as malformed PropertyShape
upstream_issues.status_vs_pr_1025: known — equivalent to Luis's Bug A
upstream_issues.suggested_fix: Lift sh:or to NodeShape level; each alternative is a full sh:property with its own sh:path and sh:minCount 1

upstream_issues.symptom: Base croissant:evaluation constraint allows only EvaluationTask, but TaskProblemShape allows EvaluationSpec; TaskProblem ⊑ Task so both shapes apply additively under RDFS inference, rejecting the documented EvaluationSpec pattern
upstream_issues.status_vs_pr_1025: appears new — not flagged in PR #1025
upstream_issues.suggested_fix: Extend base TaskShape evaluation constraint to sh:or (EvaluationTask | EvaluationSpec)

upstream_issues.symptom: sh:qualifiedMinCount 1 without sh:qualifiedValueShape on croissant:evaluatedSolution — invalid SHACL
upstream_issues.status_vs_pr_1025: known — equivalent to Luis's Bug B
upstream_issues.suggested_fix: Replace sh:qualifiedMinCount 1 with sh:minCount 1 (no companion sh:qualifiedMaxCount exists)

upstream_issues.symptom: Deeply nested sh:or → sh:property → sh:node → sh:property causes pyshacl to lose the inner sh:qualifiedValueShape
upstream_issues.status_vs_pr_1025: possibly new — not in PR #1025's findings table
upstream_issues.suggested_fix: Flatten the sh:or to two simple alternatives pyshacl can process
